Since the beginning of my IT career, I have been interested in automation, and my bachelor's degree in computer science introduced me to cloud-native technologies. I am always interested in new technologies and learning about their advantages. I enjoy working in a team because teamwork makes the dream work.

person.name: Julien Sudan
person.bio: I am Julien Sudan, a Site Reliability Engineer with extensive experience in architecting secure, scalable cloud infrastructures and implementing DevSecOps and SRE practices. Currently working at Exoscale in Switzerland, I excel in using technologies like Kubernetes, and IaC to optimize cloud deployments across environment, whether on premise or in the Cloud. I actively contribute to the cloud-native community by sharing insights through presentations at conferences and wrote articles on different blogs. My technical proficiency and commitment to continuous learning are demonstrated by a comprehensive suite of certifications, including CISSP, Kubestronaut, and several cloud-specific credentials..

person.name: Alperen Öztaş
person.bio: I'm a proud Kubestronaut and DevOps engineer passionate about Kubernetes, automation, and building secure, resilient infrastructure. I take a deep-dive approach to cloud native technologies—exploring not just how they work, but why they’re designed that way. I enjoy working on hands-on projects, maintaining on-prem clusters, and experimenting with Go-based tooling to better understand and contribute to the cloud native ecosystem.

person.name: Amine Hadri
person.bio: I am a cloud-native platform engineer designing and operating secure, multi-cluster Kubernetes platforms at enterprise scale. My work focuses on platform engineering, tenant isolation, GitOps, Crossplane, and policy-driven automation to build reliable internal developer platforms. I am passionate about production excellence, security, and enabling teams to move fast with strong architectural foundations.

person.name: Ashley Morris
person.bio: Software engineer with a passion for software documentation with 10+ years experience improving software quality by implementing processes for comprehensive automated testing solutions and for extensive software documentation. Ashley is a technical writer for HAProxy Technologies with experience in high-performance C/C++ and Python software for Linux and AWS, CI/CD, and automated QA. Through this technical lens, Ashley enjoys the challenge of testing intricate software features and configurations to find effective documentation solutions to complex problems, developing content with DevOps in mind.

person.name: Rohan Rajguru
person.bio: I am a Linux and Open Source enthusiast, still new to Kubernetes and cloud-native, and I'm loving it! I enjoy implementing the tools and concepts I've learned. These days, I’ve also started documenting my journey on LinkedIn and my blog. Let's connect!

person.name: Emmanuel Mendoza Sosa
person.bio: Emmanuel is a seasoned DevOps and Site Reliability Engineer with over a decade of experience across finance, retail, media and supply chain industries. He specializes in Cloud, Infrastructure as Code and Kubernetes, with a strong passion for collaborating with development teams to solve and debug complex problems. Emmanuel likes to learn open-source technologies that enhance and contribute to the DevOps ecosystem.

person.name: Gaber Senan
person.bio: Over 10+ years of experience in software using different technologies with solid experience in integration and middleware systems development. I gained experience in building multi tiers applications and enterprise level systems integration in different fields payment, government sectors. I participated in full system development life cycle .

person.name: Matias Alejandro Mospan
person.bio: Argentinian living in Uruguay, with over 20 years of experience in IT, migrating and evolving through various technologies. I am passionate about innovation and am constantly learning and moving forward. I have been a speaker at events like KCD Argentina and Kubeday Colombia, where I had the chance to share some of my experience. I am also part of the Buenos Aires chapter of the CNCF.  Outside of work, I am part of a beautiful family with my wife and daughters, I play Pickleball, and I'm a fan of Quilmes in football (soccer) and McLaren in Formula 1.

person.name: Santosh Kaluskar
person.bio: Driven Platform Engineer with expertise in building services that empower development teams to efficiently develop, test, deploy, and monitor applications. Specialized in Linux containers, Kubernetes, Observability, and software supply chain security. Proven track record of delivering large-scale IT infrastructure projects and transitioning into cloud-native technologies. Committed to continuous learning and building reliable, scalable, and secure infrastructure that facilitates seamless application deployment.

person.name: Damon Wright
person.bio: Senior platform engineer at Capgemini.

person.name: Matt Slater
person.bio: Experienced Azure Platform Engineer with a passion for anything Kubernetes and Containers.

person.name: Spoorthi Palakshaiah
person.bio: Spoorthi is a DevOps Engineer with a strong background in building scalable, resilient, and secure cloud-native infrastructure. Her expertise spans Kubernetes, Helm, AWS, Terraform, observability, automation, and CI/CD pipelines. Passionate about leveraging the power of data and cloud to create innovative and impactful products and services that solve real-world problems.

person.name: Jim Chen
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with nearly 4 years of experience in software development and 6+ years in consultancy and banking technology. Specialized in DevSecOps tools, enabling teams to build robust, automated CI/CD pipelines.  Passionate about integrating diverse tools across the development lifecycle, improving system reliability, security, and scalability.

person.name: Christopher Jachetti
person.bio: Christopher Jachetti is a passionate cybersecurity professional with over 20 industry certifications, including the CISSP, AWS Solutions Architect Professional, and many more. He discovered his calling in cloud security early on and has been immersed in the IT world since high school, continuously evolving his expertise to protect modern infrastructures. As a CNCF Kubestronaut, Christopher embodies a relentless drive for constant learning and improvement. He is committed to empowering organizations and individuals alike, sharing his insights and best practices to push the boundaries of DevSecOps innovation.

person.name: Joobin P Nihas
person.bio: Cloud Native Engineer with expertise in Disaster Recovery, building scalable and secure applications, and application modernization, with a strong focus on cloud-native technologies.  My journey in the technology industry began in 2015 as a web hosting support engineer. Over the years, I have developed extensive experience working with Azure and AWS, focusing on how cloud-native technologies can help organizations optimize infrastructure costs while improving operational visibility and reliability.  I regularly support enterprises by providing application modernization strategies and architectural guidance, helping them adopt industry best practices for cloud-native infrastructure and platform engineering. Over the past decade, I have gained international experience working with organizations in Dubai and India.

person.name: Nemanja Filipović
person.bio: Nemanja has been spending a good part of his life exploring how technology and psychology shape collaboration. With experience across many projects, companies, and roles, he is deeply engaged with enterprise solutions, CNCF technologies, and people-centered leadership.

person.name: Sunil Kumar Shetty
person.bio: I am a cloud engineer working at Fidelity Investments. I am passionate about learning new technologies and doing technical certifications. I excel in compute and infrastructure design and automation.

person.name: Mark Sawle
person.bio: A software engineer, turned platform engineer, I like to use my coding background to solve infrastructure and deployment problems in novel ways with an "automate everything" mindset and a love of writing quality code. In addition to being a certified AWS Solution Architect, I have 5 years of direct Kubernetes experience and more in the general area of containerisation. I've gone from running a handful of containers serving a small development team, to managing critical national infrastructure supporting millions of users, with a number of other deployments in between. I'm keen to expand my knowledge of Kubernetes and the wider ecosystem, so Kubestronaut is just the beginning!

person.name: Akram Boudhraa
person.bio: I am a DevSecOps Engineer working currently as Cloud Support Engineer for AWS (Amazon Web Services). I am specialized in Kubernetes and Containers in general, and worked on K8s managed services from major Cloud providers.
